title: DSDS Governance Metadata Field
description: Who is accountable for this entity's documentation, and when a human last confirmed it is accurate. Governance answers the question 'who do I ask when the doc contradicts the code' and lets tools flag documentation that no one has verified recently. It is distinct from `lastUpdated`: updated records when the documentation changed; reviewed records when someone confirmed it is still true. A doc untouched for a year but reviewed last month is healthy. Introduced as an initial draft in 0.12.1 — the shape may change in a future release.

owner.description: The party accountable for this entity's documentation. A bare string names the owner and covers the common case. Use the object form to add contact details. Owners SHOULD be teams, roles, or group aliases (e.g., 'Design Systems', '@acme/design-system') rather than named individuals — person-level ownership goes stale on turnover, which is the exact situation this field exists for. Tools SHOULD surface the owner wherever a reader might need to escalate a discrepancy.

oneOf.description: Name or alias of the owning team, role, or group (e.g., 'Design Systems', '@acme/design-system').
Full owner form: the owning party's name plus a contact route.

name.description: Name or alias of the owning team, role, or group (e.g., 'Design Systems'). SHOULD name a team or role, not an individual.

contact.description: How to reach the owner: a URL, email address, or channel reference (e.g., 'https://github.com/orgs/acme/teams/design-system', 'design-system@acme.com', '#design-system'). Freeform; tools SHOULD render it as a link when it parses as a URL or email.

lastReviewed.description: When a human last confirmed this entity's documentation is accurate. A bare ISO 8601 date string ('2026-06-12') covers the common case. Use the object form to record who reviewed and what they concluded. Reviewing does not imply changing: a review that finds the doc correct updates `lastReviewed` and leaves `lastUpdated` alone. Tools MAY treat an old or absent `lastReviewed` as a staleness signal, and agents SHOULD prefer recently reviewed documentation when sources conflict.

oneOf.description: ISO 8601 date (YYYY-MM-DD) of the most recent review of this entity's documentation.
Full lastReviewed form: the date plus who reviewed and an optional note on the outcome.

date.description: ISO 8601 date (YYYY-MM-DD) of the most recent review of this entity's documentation.

reviewedBy.description: Who performed the review — a team, role, or individual (e.g., 'Design Systems', 'jane.doe'). Unlike `owner`, naming an individual here is fine: it records a historical fact, not a standing responsibility.

note.description: Plain-text note on the review outcome (e.g., 'Verified against v3.1; prop table still accurate', 'Guidelines section needs a rewrite — tracked in DS-412'). MUST NOT contain markup.

governance.description: Accountability and review state for this entity's documentation. `owner` is required — a governance object that names no owner answers none of the questions the field exists for. `lastReviewed` and `reviewedAgainst` are optional but SHOULD be set once a review process exists; together they let a tool answer 'is this doc verified, against which version, and who vouches for it.'

reviewedAgainst.description: The implementation version the documentation was verified against in the most recent review, as a package version or release identifier (e.g., '@acme/ui@3.1.0', '3.1.0'). Lets tools detect drift: if the implementation has moved past this version, the documentation MAY be out of date even if recently reviewed. Meaningful only alongside `lastReviewed`.
